claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you are claiming Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim for my son Alex. He's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 18 years old, eligibility for Child Benefit depends on his current situation. You can usually only claim Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training.

Could you tell me, is Alex currently in any of the following:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, NVQs up to level 3, or similar qualifications)?
- An approved training course (such as a traineeship in England)?

Or has Alex left education/training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and hasn't continued with any courses or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Let me check the specific eligibility rules to give you an accurate assessment regarding Alex's situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the official guidance, I can now provide you with a clear answer about Alex's eligibility.

Since Alex is 18 years old and has finished school without continuing into any approved education or training, unfortunately **you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:
- Child Benefit normally covers children **under 16**
- For children aged **16-19**, you can only continue receiving Child Benefit if they stay in **approved education or training**
- Since Alex has left education and isn't in any approved training, he no longer qualifies

There is one exception worth mentioning: If Alex is 16 or 17 and has **recently** left education or training, you could apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if he registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. However, since Alex is 18, this option would not apply to him.

Let me record this assessment for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To summarise:

**Alex - Not Eligible**

Unfortunately, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex because:
- He is 18 years old
- He is not currently in approved education or training
- Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 requires them to remain in approved education or training

If Alex's circumstances change in the future—for example, if he decides to return to full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or NVQs up to level 3)—you may be able to claim again, provided he is still under 20 when he starts.

Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and has finished school without continuing into approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, you can only claim for a child aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is not in any education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria. The 20-week extension for those who leave education and register with a careers service only applies to 16 or 17 year olds, so this also does not apply to Alex at age 18.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. Alex is 18 years old and has left education without continuing into approved education or training. Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 is only available if the child remains in approved education or training, which Alex does not.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and has finished school without continuing into approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, you can only claim for a child aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is not in any education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria. The 20-week extension for those who leave education and register with a careers service only applies to 16 or 17 year olds, so this also does not apply to Alex at age 18.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. Alex is 18 years old and has left education without continuing into approved education or training. Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 is only available if the child remains in approved education or training, which Alex does not.
